metadata.title: Hold Cleaning Intelligence Knowledge Base
metadata.description: Comprehensive knowledge base on cargo hold cleaning, preparation, and regulatory compliance for ocean-going dry bulk carriers
metadata.author: William S. Davis III
metadata.context: Ocean-going dry bulk carriers (Handysize through Capesize)
zotero_sources_cross_referenced: IMSBC Code (MSC.268(85) as amended by MSC.318(89) and MSC.354(92))
Bulk Carrier Practice — Capt. J. Isbester FNI MRIN, Nautical Institute 1993
Carrying Solid Bulk Cargoes Safely — Lloyd's Register/UK P&I/Intercargo, 2nd ed. 2016
Marine Cargo Operations: A Guide to Stowage 3rd ed. — Meurn & Sauerbier, Cornell Maritime Press 2004

hierarchy.name: Hospital Clean
hierarchy.description: Most stringent standard. 100% intact paint coatings on all surfaces including tank top, all ladder rungs, and undersides of hatch covers. No loose rust, no paint flakes, no previous cargo residues anywhere.
hierarchy.rust_tolerance: ZERO - no rust of any kind
hierarchy.practical_note: Rarely achievable in tramp trades. Generally requires vessels exclusively trading clean cargoes.
hierarchy.reference: UK P&I FAQ; Bulkcarrierguide.com; Brookes Bell; West of England P&I Loss Prevention Bulletin

hierarchy.name: Grain Clean
hierarchy.description: Most commonly required standard. Clean, swept, washed down with fresh water and free from insects, odour, residue of previous cargo, loose rust scale, and paint flakes. Dried, well-ventilated and ready to receive cargo.
required_cargoes: All grains (wheat, corn, barley, oats, rye, rice as commodity)
hierarchy.usda_definition: Stowage area must be clean, dry, free of infestation, rodents, toxic substances and foreign odour. Not more than 25 sq ft of loose rust scale in any single area or 100 sq ft in aggregate.
hierarchy.ncb_definition: Compartments must be clean, dry, devoid of odors, and gas-free. All loose scale eliminated.
hierarchy.rust_tolerance: Oxidation rust (non-flaking) may be acceptable. Loose scale (breaks when struck with fist) NOT acceptable.
hierarchy.scale_tolerance: No loose scale. Oxidation rust typically acceptable.

jurisdiction_variations.USA: Particularly strict; multiple inspections; NCB certificate required for grain exports; zero tolerance in many ports
jurisdiction_variations.Australia: Zero tolerance criteria - considered among world's strictest
jurisdiction_variations.Canada: Strict parameters exceeding IGC in some cases
jurisdiction_variations.General: Varies by surveyor and port - 'band or range' rather than fixed specification

hierarchy.white_glove_test: No visible residues transferred onto white cloth when rubbed against hold surface
hierarchy.reference: USDA FGIS Section 7; NCB Grain Certificate of Readiness; UK P&I FAQ 2021; NVIC 5-94

hierarchy.name: Normal Clean
hierarchy.description: When no specific instructions in charterparty. Residues removed, holds double swept, salt water washed, fresh water rinsed, dried.
required_cargoes: General bulk cargoes where no specific CP language
hierarchy.reference: Swedish Club Hold Cleaning Guide 2023; Safety4Sea

hierarchy.name: Shovel Clean
hierarchy.description: Holds mechanically cleaned to minimum standard. Typically for dirty cargo trades.
hierarchy.reference: UK P&I Carefully to Carry 2023 Ch.17

hierarchy.name: Load on Top
hierarchy.description: Cargo loaded directly on top of existing residues from previous identical cargo. Used in continuous commodity trades (e.g. coal COA).

phase_1_immediate_post_discharge.name: Immediate Action (During/After Discharge)
phase_1_immediate_post_discharge.timing: Begin before vessel finishes discharge where possible
steps: Insist stevedores discharge as thoroughly as possible - document residues with photos
Letter of protest if discharge incomplete
Remove dunnage, lashing materials, timber, securing gear
Initial dry sweep - remove bulk residues before any water
Open all accessible bilge covers and clean bilge wells of previous cargo
Check and document hold condition photographically

phase_2_dry_cleaning.name: Dry Cleaning Phase
steps: Thorough mechanical sweeping of all surfaces - tank top, frames, hoppers
Air blowing if local regulations permit (very effective for dust cargoes)
Scraping of frame knees, tripping brackets, behind ladders, pipe guards
High areas: underdeck frames and coaming frames (often only accessible by scaffold tower or cherry picker)
Hatch cover undersides - sweep all residues from cover beams
Collect all residues into containers for disposal per MARPOL Annex V
Bilge strainers/filters must remain in place throughout
critical_areas: Upper underdeck frames (inaccessible without scaffold on large vessels)
Bilge well covers and bilge wells
Tank top corners and hopper sloped surfaces

coal_petcoke.challenge: Black dust + oily smear films; staining on ledges
coal_petcoke.recommended: Heavy-duty alkaline cleaner (hydroxide base + non-ionic surfactants). Wilhelmsen Unitor CargoClean HD or Navadan equivalent.
coal_petcoke.application: Apply 10% concentration with Panamax kit foam applicator. Leave 10-15 min wet. High-pressure washdown from bottom up.
coal_petcoke.marpol_status: CargoClean HD: NOT HME - may be discharged to sea per MARPOL Annex V

cement_clinker.challenge: Hydration with water creates concrete; clogs bilge systems
cement_clinker.warning: DO NOT use bilge pump during cement cleaning - cement hardens in pipelines
cement_clinker.recommended: Portable salvage pump for wash water removal. Thorough dry sweep before any water contact.
cement_clinker.special: Fresh water wash only after thorough sweeping

sulphur.challenge: Wet dust + sulphurous acid (highly corrosive to personnel and steel)
sulphur.recommended: Personnel: full PPE, goggles, facemasks throughout. Thorough drying after washing.
sulphur.imsbc_note: IMSBC Code: holds must be clean and dry and thoroughly washed with fresh water

fishmeal_organic_odorous.recommended: Enzymatic cleaner (protease/amylase + surfactant). Dwell time critical. Warm water improves performance. Maximum ventilation.
fishmeal_organic_odorous.note: Oxidizer only if justified and fully rinsed

ore_concentrates.recommended: Acid descaler (phosphoric/citric blend + corrosion inhibitors). Neutralizer carbonate rinse after acid stage. Mechanical break-up first.
ore_concentrates.sequence: Mechanical → controlled acid treatment → neutralize → full rinse → dry

copper_concentrate.challenge: CRITICAL WARNING: Copper concentrate MUST NOT BE WASHED with water. If washed, it forms a 'concrete'-like layer bonded to the hold sides which can only be removed with abrasive discs on disc sander/grinders.
copper_concentrate.correct_method: Thoroughly blow out with compressed air and sweep clean. NO water contact.
copper_concentrate.source: Isbester, Bulk Carrier Practice Ch.5 p.71 — verified by Nautical Institute/multiple P&I clubs
copper_concentrate.marpol_note: Dry residues collected and disposed per MARPOL Annex V — not permitted to discharge at sea.

petcoke_stubborn_patches.challenge: After main wash, greasy or discoloured patches remain on hold surfaces
petcoke_stubborn_patches.procedure: Hand scrub discoloured patches with household detergent using stiff brush, THEN wash with portable high-pressure washing machine (350-500 bar), THEN rewash entire hold.
petcoke_stubborn_patches.note: Petcoke staining requires extended washing time vs coal. Shore gang may be required for thorough chemical treatment.
petcoke_stubborn_patches.source: Isbester, Bulk Carrier Practice Ch.5 p.71

application_equipment.Unitor_Panamax_Kit: Standard foam application for typical Panamax/post-Panamax holds
application_equipment.Navadan_Ultimate_Kit: Best performance with CargoClean HD for heaviest contamination
application_equipment.Navadan_Essential_Kit: For smaller areas or spot cleaning
application_equipment.Tornado_3: Air-powered water cannon; reaches 30m height; foam or plain water nozzle
application_equipment.Combi_Gun: High pressure air/water gun for upper reaches; standard on well-equipped bulkers
application_equipment.Chemical_Applicator_150: 150L pressure tank for chemical application with long-reach lance

washing_methods.source: Isbester, Bulk Carrier Practice Ch.5 pp.70-72

method_1_handheld_hose.name: Handheld Hose
method_1_handheld_hose.description: Water drawn from sea via eductors or bilge pumps, discharged overboard. Team of 2-3 seamen: one controls deck valve, others drag hose and direct jet around hold.
method_1_handheld_hose.best_for: Small ships and holds that are not particularly dirty. Quickest and best method for small vessels.
method_1_handheld_hose.limitation: Less satisfactory in larger ships — difficult to achieve good cleaning in remote hold areas; labor-intensive; requires large crew.

method_2_water_cannon.name: Water Cannon (Combi-Gun / High-Pressure)
method_2_water_cannon.description: Hose led to high-pressure sea-water cannon (e.g. Combi-gun) on tripod placed in hold. Uses compressed air from ship's deck line to inject greater pressure into fire main water.
method_2_water_cannon.best_for: Larger bulk carriers; provides more powerful jet; results in better cleaning of high extremities.
method_2_water_cannon.limitation: Takes longer to move than handheld hose; requires same labor.

method_3_permanent_installation.name: Permanent Washing Installation
method_3_permanent_installation.description: Water guns installed under deckhead in hold. Full washing programme selected, gun automatically moves through hatch cover → deckhead → bulkheads → tanktop. Alternative bottom-wash programme for tanktop only.
method_3_permanent_installation.best_for: Mini-bulkers and smaller vessels; designed to wash with hatches closed; can be used at sea.
method_3_permanent_installation.note: No labor required during wash cycle. Designed to wash toward aft end of hold where bilge suctions are located.

order: 1. Hatch covers — top, bottom, and sides
2. Compression bars and rubbers (scrub if cargo adhering)
5. Hold sides — working down, paying particular attention to hopper angles, pipe guards, brackets, and other non-vertical surfaces
9. Flush bilges — ensure suctions clear throughout
washing_sequence.salt_water_caveat: Salt traces remain after seawater wash. Unacceptable to grain surveyors; also contaminates steel products and woodpulp and encourages corrosion. Fresh water rinse mandatory after seawater wash for salt-sensitive cargoes.
washing_sequence.port_restrictions_warning: INCREASING NUMBER OF PORTS prohibit hold washing within port limits. Obtain written permission from port authority via agent BEFORE washing. A verbal assurance from a stevedore is NO guarantee. Written permission required.

phase_4_seawater_wash.name: Salt Water Wash
steps: High-pressure seawater washdown of all surfaces
Direct from lowest point upward (bottom to top)
Ensure bilge well strainers in place - never remove during washing
Check bilge pump frequently - clear strainers as needed to prevent blockage
Pump bilge water overboard ONLY if compliant with MARPOL Annex V position and cargo classification

phase_5_freshwater_rinse.name: Fresh Water Final Rinse
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.critical_importance: CRITICAL - removes chlorides that cause: (a) salt contamination of sensitive cargo, (b) coating breakdown, (c) corrosion, (d) receivers alleging sea water ingress based on silver nitrate chloride tests
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.timing: Before hold dries after seawater wash - salt easier to remove when surfaces still wet
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.quantity_guide: Typical 110,000 DWT bulk carrier: ~30 tonnes fresh water per hatch. Store in aft peak tank, pump to fire main via GS pump. A handy-size vessel (Regina Oldendorff type) with 200 t/hr fire pump uses ~50 t total for entire ship in 2-3 min per hold (Isbester).
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.portable_hpwm_alternative: If insufficient ship water supply: portable high-pressure washing machine — uses less fresh water but takes much longer.
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.procedure: Flush fire main with fresh water first to remove salt from piping. Close all deck hydrants and anchor washers.
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.charterparty_clause: When fresh water wash is required by cargo type (grain, steel, woodpulp), receipt for fresh water should be claused 'Hold Washing — For Charterer's Account'. This is accepted practice in some trades. (Isbester Ch.5 p.71)
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.note: Even a 2-3 minute rinse removes most salt deposits. Fresh water washing recommended for all grain-bound holds but cost often underestimated — factor into PDA.
phase_5_freshwater_rinse.source: Isbester, Bulk Carrier Practice Ch.5 pp.70-72; Skuld Loss Prevention 2014

phase_6_drying_ventilation.name: Drying and Ventilation
steps: Maximum forced ventilation to dry completely
Check daily - holds must be absolutely dry before loading grain or soda ash
Remove all moisture from bilge covers, hatch coaming areas, and low points
Check hatch cover drainage channels and rubbers

phase_7_protective_barrier_optional.name: Protective Barrier Application (Pre-Loading for Difficult Cargoes)
phase_7_protective_barrier_optional.purpose: Applied before loading dirty cargoes to make next cleaning easier

products.Unitor_Slip_Coat: Water-soluble film. Use for DRY cargoes. Coal, coke, petcoke, ore, minerals. NOT for cargoes with water content (sulphur). Covers 15-20 m2/ltr. Dry 1-2 hours before loading.
products.Navadan_BarrierHold: Water-resistant film. Use for WET, corrosive, or humid-condition cargoes. Coal, coke, petcoke. Ideal when condensation expected. Drying time 1-2 hours.

not_for: Grain (must be hospital or grain clean)
Soda ash (hospital clean - no chemicals; fresh water only)

grain.cleanliness_required: Grain Clean (minimum); some ports/surveyors require higher
regulatory_framework: International Grain Code (SOLAS Ch.VI mandatory since Jan 1, 1994)
pre_loading_requirements: Document of Authorization (DOA) for grain stability - required on all grain ships
Grain Loading Manual / Stability Booklet (NCB-stamped for US exports)
NCB Grain Certificate of Readiness (mandatory for US export grain)
Details of at least previous THREE cargoes required by surveyor
hold_specific_requirements: Bilge covers grain-tight (NOT watertight - must drain)
Two layers clean hessian cloth on bilge strainer plate
Duct tape over gap between bilge strainer and tank top
Hold hydrant area cleaned, dried, steel cover refitted
ncb_survey_checks: Protection against heat and electrical hazards
loading_considerations: All holds must be completely filled - trim to all corners
Allow grain to settle, refill voids
Grain cargo must remain dry throughout - rain/moisture = major claim
Dust clouds during loading: PPE required (goggles, masks)
Dyeing agent may be required for animal consumption grain (mixed in water, sprayed)
Steam heating of adjacent oil tanks must be minimized during voyage
Angle of repose and trimming stability must be calculated

fumigation.trigger: Any signs of insect infestation, larvae, eggs
fumigation.treatment: Approved fumigant; holds sealed; typically 12-hour build-up period
fumigation.departure: Vessel must NOT depart before minimum fumigation period expires

grain.fresh_water_requirement: ~30 tonnes per hatch for 110,000 DWT vessel

soda_ash.cleanliness_required: Hospital Clean - EXCEEDS grain clean standard
soda_ash.imsbc_classification: Group C (non-hazardous) - but extreme purity requirement
hold_requirements: Hospital clean: watertight, dried, clean in ALL respects
Free of scale, loose rust, ALL foreign materials
Free of residue of previous cargo on: tank tops, bulkheads, hatch coamings, underside of hatch covers
NO bubble rust, NO loose rust, NO painted rust that can be chipped/scraped
Ventilation ports/fan spaces checked for loose rust or paint chips
ALL surfaces must be free of contamination - paintwork in GOOD condition
critical_restrictions: DO NOT chemically wash holds before soda ash - contamination risk from cleaning agents
Fresh water (high pressure) ONLY for hold cleaning
No chemical cleaning agents of any kind
Surveyor inspection exceeds grain clean stringency
cargo_properties: Highly hygroscopic (absorbs moisture readily) - must remain ABSOLUTELY DRY
Dust hazard: protect machinery, equipment, accommodation from dust
PPE required: goggles and dust filter masks on deck throughout operations
loading_operations: Weather routing critical - avoid rain during loading
Hatch covers to be closed promptly if rain threatens

considerations: Some grades give off methane - O2 depletion risk, enclosed space hazard
Check IMSBC Code group (A, B, or C) - Group A/B require special precautions
Petcoke: very high alkalinity; use heavy-duty alkaline cleaners afterwards
coal.post_coal_cleaning_note: Coal and petcoke staining difficult; requires CargoClean HD or equivalent alkaline degreaser. Black-to-white cargo change is most demanding cleaning scenario.

critical_warnings: NEVER use bilge pump during cement cleaning - hardens in pipes
Use portable salvage pump for wash water
Dry sweep thoroughly before any water contact
Any residue + water = concrete in bilge lines

considerations: Some fertilizers HME under MARPOL - check classification before discharging wash water
Ammonium nitrate: oxidizing; strict IMSBC requirements
MOP/SOP (potash products): not HME typically but verify

marpol_annex_v.title: MARPOL 73/78 Annex V - Prevention of Pollution by Garbage from Ships
marpol_annex_v.key_principle: Default prohibition on all garbage discharge at sea, with narrow exceptions
marpol_annex_v.cargo_residue_definition: Remnants of any cargo remaining on deck or in holds after loading/unloading, including excess, spillage, in wet or dry condition or entrained in wash water. EXCLUDES dust on external surfaces.

hme_classification.title: Harmful to Marine Environment (HME)
hme_classification.determination: Based on GESAMP Hazard Profiles, country UN GHS databases, IMSBC Code
hme_classification.consequence_hme: PROHIBITED from discharge at sea in ANY form - must go to port reception facility
hme_classification.consequence_non_hme: May be discharged under specific conditions (see below)

outside_special_areas.condition_1: Ship must be EN ROUTE (underway, not at anchor)
outside_special_areas.condition_2: Minimum 12 nautical miles from nearest land or ice shelf
outside_special_areas.condition_3: As far as practicable from nearest land
outside_special_areas.condition_4: Residues must be non-recoverable using commonly available unloading methods
outside_special_areas.condition_5: Cargo residues + cleaning agents must BOTH be non-HME
outside_special_areas.wash_water_note: Cargo residues entrained in wash water treated as cargo residues

areas: Wider Caribbean Region (incl. Gulf of Mexico)
inside_special_areas.narrow_exception: Only if: (1) departure AND destination ports both within same Special Area, (2) no transit outside Special Area, (3) no adequate reception facilities at either port
inside_special_areas.if_exception_applies: Discharge non-HME residues as far to sea as practicable, min 12nm from nearest land

cleaning_agents_discharge.rule: May be discharged if NOT classified as HME
cleaning_agents_discharge.documentation_required: Supplier signed/dated statement that product is not HME (part of MSDS or standalone). Ship maintains records.
cleaning_agents_discharge.example_compliant: Wilhelmsen Unitor CargoClean HD - confirmed NOT HME per MARPOL Annex V
cleaning_agents_discharge.example_non_compliant: Products containing sodium hypochlorite (bleach) - may be HME

record_keeping.garbage_record_book: Part I (all ships) + Part II (ships carrying solid bulk cargo)
record_keeping.required_entries: Date/time, position (lat/long) or port, category, estimated amount, officer signature, start/stop positions for discharge
record_keeping.note: Can be maintained electronically with proper approval

enforcement_risk.us_enforcement: Criminal liability for individual officers, not just company. Whistleblower programs with rewards exceeding $1M.
enforcement_risk.surveillance: Advanced satellite technology now detects illegal dumping
enforcement_risk.port_authority: Some ports require lab analysis of hold wash water before permitting any discharge

marpol_annex_v.bimco_hme_clause: BIMCO HME Cargo Residues Disposal Clause for Voyage Charter - shifts HME disposal responsibility/cost to charterer. Strongly recommended for HME or potential HME cargoes.

international_grain_code.title: International Code for the Safe Carriage of Grain in Bulk
international_grain_code.mandated_by: SOLAS Chapter VI Part C Regulation 9 (Resolution MSC.23[59])
international_grain_code.applies_to: All vessels loading grain in bulk in US waters (with some inland waterway exceptions)
requirements: Document of Authorization (DOA) - must be onboard
Compliance with specific stability criteria for loaded, voyage, and discharge stages
Consideration of: angle of repose, trimming, surface heeling moments

us_specific.ncb_role: NCB (National Cargo Bureau) empowered by USCG to approve grain loading calculations. Cannot approve until cleanliness certificate issued.
us_specific.nvic_5_94: USCG Navigation and Vessel Inspection Circular 5-94: Requirements for US Vessels obtaining Documents of Authorization
us_specific.nvic_3_97: USCG NVIC 3-97: ABS authorized to review stability booklets for grain ships

imsbc_code.title: International Maritime Solid Bulk Cargoes Code
imsbc_code.mandated_by: SOLAS Chapter VI Regulation 1-1, mandatory from 1 January 2011
imsbc_code.grain_exception: IMPORTANT: The transport of grain is covered by the INTERNATIONAL GRAIN CODE, not the IMSBC Code. Grain ships must comply with the Grain Code separately.
imsbc_code.relevance: Specifies hold cleanliness requirements by cargo. Determines HME status. Many cargoes listed as 'clean and dry as relevant to hazards' or 'no special requirements'. Each cargo schedule in Appendix 1 includes a HOLD CLEANLINESS field.

cargo_groups.Group_A: Cargoes that may liquefy if shipped at moisture content exceeding their Transportable Moisture Limit (TML). Examples: nickel ore, mineral concentrates, iron ore fines.
cargo_groups.Group_B: Cargoes that possess a chemical hazard (toxic gas emission, self-heating, flammability). Examples: coal (primary classification), sulphur.
cargo_groups.Group_C: Cargoes that are neither liable to liquefy (A) nor possess chemical hazards (B). Examples: iron ore (coarse), grain (covered by Grain Code separately).
cargo_groups.dual_classification_note: Coal is most commonly Group B, but can be BOTH Group A and Group B if 75% or more of the cargo by mass consists of particles less than 5mm in size (fine coal). This affects handling and moisture testing requirements.

imsbc_code.hold_cleanliness_requirement: IMSBC Code Section 2.2.1: Cargo spaces shall be inspected and prepared for the particular cargo which is to be loaded. Section 2.2.2: Due consideration shall be paid to bilge wells and strainer plates. Section 2.2.3: Bilge lines, sounding pipes and other service lines shall be in good order.
imsbc_code.shipper_obligations: Shippers must provide cargo declarations including HME classification per IMSBC Section 4. Misdeclared cargoes pose catastrophic risks (liquefaction, self-heating casualties).

nype_form.form: New York Produce Exchange (NYPE) - standard time charter form
nype_form.key_clause_13: Vessel on delivery shall be ready to receive cargo with clean-swept holds
nype_form.crew_obligation: Customary assistance includes standard hold cleaning (sweeping, washing residues)
nype_form.off_hire_trigger: Vessel may be placed off hire if holds fail inspection
nype_form.case_law_banga_saga_lima: Charterers lost right to claim cleaning costs by not insisting on grain clean at first load port even though first cargo (iron ore) didn't require it

voyage_charter.owners_account: Removal and disposal of cargo residues normally for owners' account under standard voyage charter
voyage_charter.exception: With BIMCO HME clause - shifts to charterer for HME cargoes

scenarios: Failed hold inspection before grain loading
Delay in remediating deficiencies after failed inspection
Extra cleaning costs if crew cannot clean further (some ports prohibit crew cleaning)
Shore cleaning gang required at premium cost

key_publications.title: Carefully to Carry 2023 - Chapter 17: Hold Preparation and Cleaning
Carefully to Carry 2023 - Chapter 1: Preparation, Survey and Load
Bulk Matters - UK P&I Grain Loading Guide
Guidance on Preparing Cargo Holds and Loading of Solid Bulk Cargoes
Guidance on Disposal of Cargo Residues in line with MARPOL Annex V
Cargo Hold Cleaning - Loss Prevention Bulletin
Bulk Cargoes - Hold Preparation and Cleaning
Practical Guide: Hold Cleaning of Bulk Vessels (with CWA International)
Discharge of Cargo Residues into the Sea under MARPOL Annex V

key_references.title: MARPOL Annex V - Prevention of Pollution by Garbage from Ships
NVIC 5-94 - Requirements for US Vessels Obtaining Documents of Authorization (Grain Code)
NVIC 3-97 - ABS Authorization for Grain Stability Review
Federal Grain Inspection Service - Stowage Examination Services Directive Section 7
key_references.note: Defines grain clean standard for US ports

regulatory_bodies.name: National Cargo Bureau (NCB)
regulatory_bodies.role: USCG-authorized surveyor for grain loading compliance

key_services.title: Grain Certificate of Readiness
key_services.description: Required for all US grain exports. NCB surveyor inspects: cleanliness, bilges, structure, stability, moveable bulkheads.

primary_textbooks.title: Bulk Carrier Practice: A Practical Guide
primary_textbooks.author: Captain J. Isbester FNI MRIN (Extra Master)
primary_textbooks.relevance: THE authoritative operational reference for bulk carrier hold cleaning and preparation. Chapter 5 (pp.69-77): Preparation of Holds covers every aspect of the cleaning process in detail. Chapter 6 covers ship systems that must be operational. Used by masters, chief mates, surveyors, and consultants worldwide.

key_chapters.Ch5: Preparation of Holds — sweeping, washing methods, drying, bilge prep, hold inspections, time estimates, limewashing, ballast holds
key_chapters.Ch12: The Loading Period — preloading surveys, duties of ship's officers
key_chapters.Ch14: Cargo Documents — hold inspection certificates, fitness certificates, fumigation, empty hold certificates
key_chapters.Ch19: Carriage of Common Bulk Cargoes — grain, coal, iron ore, steel, forest products

primary_textbooks.title: Marine Cargo Operations: A Guide to Stowage, Third Edition
primary_textbooks.relevance: Chapter 4: Planning the Stowage and Preparing the Cargo Spaces (p.4-20: Preparation of Cargo Spaces; p.4-28: Checking the Condition of the Cargo Hold). Appendix 3: Stowage of Bulk Grain (contributed by Captain James J. McNamara, President of National Cargo Bureau — key NCB reference).
primary_textbooks.note: First edition 1956. Third edition 2004. Standard US maritime academy textbook.

imo_publications.title: MARPOL Consolidated Edition 2022
imo_publications.relevance: Annex V (pp.255-272): Prevention of Pollution by Garbage from Ships — governs all hold wash water discharge. Contains HME classification rules, Special Area regulations, Garbage Record Book Part II requirements.

imo_publications.title: IMSBC Code — International Maritime Solid Bulk Cargoes Code
imo_publications.relevance: Sections 1-2 cover general provisions, hold cleanliness requirements, and cargo space preparation. Section 4 covers cargo information (shipper declarations). Appendix 1 contains individual cargo schedules each with HOLD CLEANLINESS field.
imo_publications.grain_note: Grain carriage is NOT covered by IMSBC — see International Grain Code separately.

imo_publications.title: Carrying Solid Bulk Cargoes Safely — Guidance for Crews on the IMSBC Code
imo_publications.publisher: Lloyd's Register, UK P&I Club, Intercargo
imo_publications.relevance: Chapter 1: Hold inspection and preparation requirements. Chapter 2: Three cargo groups with detailed examples. Chapter 4: Cargo residues harmful to marine environment. Checklist and flowchart for accepting and loading solid bulk cargoes.

technical_service_providers.name: Wilhelmsen Ships Service
technical_service_providers.specialty: Hold cleaning chemicals and equipment (Unitor / Navadan brands)
key_products: Unitor CargoClean HD - heavy duty alkaline cleaner (coal/petcoke)
Navadan Panamax Kit, Ultimate Kit, Essential Kit, Tornado Kit

technical_service_providers.name: CWA International
technical_service_providers.specialty: Marine surveying, cargo advice, joint publication with Swedish Club

technical_service_providers.name: Brookes Bell
technical_service_providers.specialty: Marine surveyors, hold cleanliness consultancy, expert witnesses

technical_service_providers.name: CARGOWARD Maritime Limited
technical_service_providers.specialty: Hold cleaning SOP development, QA gate framework

youtube_resources.title: Bulk Carrier Hold Cleaning Prior to Grain Loading
youtube_resources.source: Referenced in ShipsNostalgia.com forum - community verified
youtube_resources.note: Original link from forum post - verify availability

youtube_resources.search_query: bulk carrier hold cleaning grain survey
suggested_searches: bulk carrier hold cleaning grain survey
cargo hold inspection survey bulk carrier
hold cleaning after coal grain preparation
Wilhelmsen Unitor cargo hold cleaning procedure
soda ash bulk carrier hold preparation
grain certificate of readiness NCB inspection
MARPOL Annex V cargo residues bulk carrier

survey_failures: Excessive loose rust scale (fails USDA 25 sq ft / single area rule)
Previous cargo residues in frames, pipe guards, behind ladders
Salt residues from seawater wash without fresh water rinse
Odour or taint from previous cargo or paint chemicals
Wet or damp holds (must be completely dry)
Damaged bilge covers or non-operational bilge suction
Loose paint flakes anywhere in hold
Contamination in hatch cover drain channels
High areas not cleaned (underdeck frames, top of hoppers)
cargo_claims: Water ingress (most common - hatch cover seal failures, ballast tank leaks)
Contamination from loose rust/paint scale falling during voyage
Moisture damage during loading (wet holds or wet cargo)
Cargo heating from adjacent steam-heated oil tanks

equipment_onboard_essentials: Combi Gun or equivalent (air/water) - ESSENTIAL for upper reaches on all bulk carriers
High-pressure cold water cleaner (350-500 bar minimum)
Scaffolding tower or cherry picker access equipment
Portable salvage pump (for cement and similar cargoes)
Chemical applicator kit appropriate to vessel size
Waste collection receptacles for residue removal
PPE: coveralls, boots, gloves, goggles, dust masks, safety harnesses

timing_and_planning.planning_start: Well before vessel arrives at discharge port - review voyage instructions, CP, next cargo requirements

isbester_time_benchmarks.source: Isbester, Bulk Carrier Practice Ch.5 p.74 — verified by Nautical Institute; these are the industry reference benchmarks

handy_panamax_normal_clean.note: Includes sweeping, washing, bilge prep. Cleaning to grain standard takes longer.

mini_bulker_grain_standard.note: Routine hold wash + bilge well rinse for mini-bulker: 1-2 hours

isbester_time_benchmarks.parallel_work: Normally only possible to wash one hold at a time, though other tasks in adjacent hold can be completed whilst washing is in progress, if sufficient labour is available.
isbester_time_benchmarks.pda_application: Use these benchmarks as floor for days_lost estimates. Higher-risk previous cargoes (coal, petcoke, fishmeal) should use upper multipliers. Shore gangs work faster but cost more.

black_to_white_estimate.scenario: Post-Panamax bulk carrier, coal to grain, 7 holds, 2-3 days ballast voyage
black_to_white_estimate.description: Extremely challenging. Some holds may be started during discharge. Requires all hands, all equipment, optimal chemical and equipment inventory.
black_to_white_estimate.hold_dimensions_reference: Post-Panamax hold: 22m tank top to hatch cover, 38m wide, 26m long = ~16,000-17,000 m3 each

shore_cleaning_triggers: Time constraints - not enough ballast voyage time
Some ports: crew NOT permitted to continue cleaning if failed inspection
Vessel type requires extensive scaffold access
timing_and_planning.crew_hours_of_rest: MLC and STCW rest hour requirements must be respected - factor into cleaning timeline

isbester_hold_preparation_checklist.source: Isbester, Bulk Carrier Practice Ch.5 p.77 — Items for attention when preparing holds for cargo (Nautical Institute)
isbester_hold_preparation_checklist.note: This is the industry-standard master checklist. Used directly to populate HoldIntel CLI checklist command.
items: Encourage stevedores to discharge cargo sweepings as far as possible
Have crew or stevedores sweep down deckheads and bulkheads as discharge proceeds (e.g. for grain cargoes)
If cargo has filled a bilge, have crew clean it before completion of discharge so contents can be discharged
Sweep holds before washing to remove bulky cargo residues, if required

Determine whether holds will NOT be washed: (same cargo again + charterers want no cleaning; clean cargo like steel coils has been carried; freezing conditions prohibit washing; vessel will remain in areas where discharge of washings is not allowed)
Decide if full wash or bottom wash required; if washing to be done by automated installation, handheld hose, or water cannon
Decide if washing is to be done at berth, within port limits, or outside limits — taking into account restrictions on discharge of cargo residues and anticipated weather/sea state
Obtain written permission from port authority to discharge hold washings if intending to wash in port

Wash holds: hatch covers (top, bottom, sides); scrub compression bars & rubbers; wash hatch coamings; wash hold deckheads; wash hold sides (hopper angles, pipe guards, brackets, non-vertical surfaces); scrub and rewash stubborn dirt; wash deck; scrape loose rust scale; flush bilges; rinse with fresh water when possible
Dry holds by ventilating, opening holds and/or mopping up puddles as necessary
Sweep holds instead of washing when washing is not necessary
Scrape and sweep holds and lift residues from hold when washing is not possible
Clean and disinfect bilge wells
Flush sounding pipes and thermometer pipes
Test bilge suctions if not already used for washing
Test bilge non-return valves, when fitted
Test bilge high level alarms, when fitted
Inspect holds for cleanliness, insect infestation, leakage and damage — remedy defects where necessary
Wrap bilge cover plates with burlap and seal with tape or cement
Reseal any manhole covers which have been opened or disturbed
Isolate hold lighting and lighting in compartments connected to the holds when this precaution is required for intended cargo
Limewash bulkheads and tanktop if required for next cargo (salt cargo, sulphur)
If holds are painted or touched up before foodstuffs cargo, ensure paint compliance certificate can be produced for the paint used
In ballast holds: close and secure cover plates for ballast suctions; open bilge suctions and CO2 injection lines

limewash_application.source: Isbester, Bulk Carrier Practice Ch.5 p.75
when_required: Before loading salt (sodium chloride) — prevents rusty surfaces from discolouring the salt
Before loading sulphur — prevents rust staining and chemical interaction with hold surfaces
limewash_application.recipe: 1 part slaked lime (hydrated calcium hydroxide — Ca(OH)₂) to 3 parts fresh water
limewash_application.mixing: Mix in 200-litre drum in the hold
limewash_application.application: Apply to bulkheads and tanktop with portable sump pump hose. Cover all surfaces to the height cargo is expected to reach.
limewash_application.drying_time: Limewash dries within a few hours
limewash_application.note: Do NOT limewash to deckhead level unless cargo will reach that high — unnecessary work. The aim is bulkheads and tanktop with a good, thick, even coat.
